MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LA CASA DE LAS MADRES' Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition

Motion granted in part; denied in part. The Court notes that the parties had agreed that Plaintiff's continued deposition would be scheduled for half a day. While it is true that 3 hours 42 minutes eclipsed on the deposition clock, the record shows that Defendant was not aware until the day of the deposition that Plaintiff needed to leave at exactly 1:30 p.m. to return to work. There was also extensive colloquy between counsel. The Court finds that it is appropriate to allow an additional 60 minutes of examination. The deposition shall occur at a mutually convenient date no later than July 24, 2020 and shall be taken via a remote deposition service. Both counsel shall avoid any excessive colloquy. Sanctions denied.

The prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 to Judge Pro Tem David McDonald by email at davidmcdonald@prodigy.net.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Lohr, Esq. 415-683-7947 for Plaintiff.
Sean Ryan Broderick, Esq. 415-278-1427 of Davis Wang for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT LA CASA DE LAS MADRES' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant La Casa De Las Madres demurrer to amended complaint is OVERRULED. The court liberally construes the amended complaint pursuant to CCP 452. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts showing that defendant failed to accommodate plaintiff and ultimately terminated her employment because of her pregnancy. (See Amended Complaint, pars. 18-23.) These facts plead discrimination. The court overrules the demurrer to the harassment claim. Paragraphs 17 and 35 sufficiently allege harassing conduct. A factual issue remains whether defendant's conduct was sufficiently severe, pervasive, or hostile to constitute harassment. The court overrules the demurrer to the failure to prevent claim given this analysis. The court overrules the demurrer to the disability discrimination cause of action. "In the context of disability discrimination, the plaintiff initially has the burden to establish a prima facie case of discrimination. The plaintiff can meet this burden by presenting evidence that demonstrates, even circumstantially or by inference, that he or she (1) suffered from a disability, or was regarded as suffering from a disability; (2) could perform the essential duties of the job with or without reasonable accommodations, and (3) was subjected to an adverse employment action because of the disability or perceived disability." (Sandell v. Taylor-Listug, Inc. (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 297, 310.) In this case, plaintiff's pregnancy was the disability (Amended Complaint pars. 14-19), plaintiff could perform the job with an accommodation (Amended Complaint, par. 19) and plaintiff was subject to an adverse employment action (Amended Complaint, pars. 23-24). The court overrules the demurrers to the failure to accommodate and interactive process claims. Defendant misconstrues paragraph 21. The court overrules the demurrer to the wrongful termination cause of action because the FEHA claims are viable. The court overrules the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. (See Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 288.) The court overrules the demurrer to the misrepresentation cause of action. Defendant presumptively knows what misrepresentations its agent made to prospective employers.

Order signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)
